comment.body:
my point here is @Sebb that i'm always suspicious about books and ppl that have any worldview. 
bcs having a well-defined worldview is like stopping your thinking as i said earlier. 
i think, reaching the point where one recognizes the only thing that can be true (knowing nothing) is the ultimate goal. 
then, as one of the poet wrote: what i have been holding so long, now i let it fall; what i have known for long, I forget"
the evidences tear apart in front of the infinity and the understanding of absurdity.
and then the mind becomes free from boundaries.
but this is a dynamic process. so it requires constant re-thinking and re-recognization. bcs out brain doesn't like "blind/empty-spaces", plotholes.

and so if you reached that recognition, the docta ignoratia... and i mean, for real.. if there is God, he or it will give you something or not. (you can't force it, and you should be careful of 'imagining'/'hallucinating' and answer from god - the saints warn us about that)

but you live by something that ultimately certainly real, without illusions... dynamically.
bcs being free from illusions is also an illusion :))

comment.body: docta ignoratia is much deeper than one would think at first. it is not a renunciation of learning, nor a declaration that science is useless. quite the opposite. It is the recognition that we need to come to know everything that exists and can arise within our system , simply bcs this is how we can discover what is NOT the truth.

comment.body:
by this I mean that anything whose truth value you can derive cannot be the "absolute truth". 
Why? bcs you derive that truth value through a chosen logical system, from a specific set of axioms. now, it is provable and easily demonstrable that every logical system we create is necessarily incomplete, and every axiom system is necessarily finite. therefore, nothing that can be derived from an axiom system along any logical pathway can ever be equivalent to absolute truth. 
we determine the value of any given statement based on only what percentage of the phenomena we know it applies to

comment.body:
if you imagine a single object floating in completely empty space, can you determine its motion? No. the reason is that motion by itself is always measured against another designated point. That is its very definition. Motion is a relative concept.
If we place two objects in empty space and they are motionless relative to each other, is it possible that they are both moving together somewhere at the same speed in a parallel vector? Yes, it is possible. But does this have any meaning? No, because again you have no reference point whatsoever against which you could measure their motion.
If they are moving toward each other, can you say which object is moving toward which? No. Every variation is possible, and there is no point in trying to figure it out.
And if there were no objects at all in empty space, could you say how big the space is?
So if you now put down your laptop, lie down on your bed, close your eyes and imagine all this, you will see that the concept of the “absolute” is meaningless

comment.body:
my favorite mathematical physicist are 
1). maxwell 
2). einstein 
3). dirac 

each had an elegance to the formulas. to the point that, one day Dirac wanted everything into one equation, on same footing. 
made no progress until he extended pauli's 2D spinor matrices into four dimensions, ie the math broke until he created four dimensional momentum vector (3D space + 1D time). 

very smart guy. it was perfect and elegant! but it produced double the answers, like how sqr(4) = +2 and -2. 

rather than throw out the silly -2 extra solution, he realized , this could actually occur in nature, if the sign flips. [charge x mass x  time] 
he concludes the paper, stating : that there MUST be a particle of equal mass to the electron, but of opposite charge, a so called the 'positron", OR mathematics does not actually describe the physical world.

1929 published ,

1931 the positron is discovered , earning him nobel prize & a million cash.

comment.body:
That is why Socrates said that wisdom is like the sky. Climb the highest mountain, and you have not become any closer to it.
It is the same with rational functions as well. The parabola seemingly approaches the origin, but never actually reaches it. And because decimal fractions exist, the next realization is that the distance remains infinite all the way.
It is the same with God and with truth as well. From our own system we cannot step out into the meta-world, because a singularity separates us. But this must be recognized, and we can only reach its boundary in an indirect way. That is why humility is necessary.

But we are already seeing this. And it didn’t even require AI. 
This is a simple, easily observable psychic phenomenon. Enormous amounts of energy are stagnating in people. You no longer have to go down for bread, the courier brings it. You no longer have to go to the bookstore, you no longer have to go to school… because it’s all online, the trains are faster… And now, thanks to AI, you don’t even have to learn languages or type things out, etc. All of this frees up time that most people simply don’t know what to do with.
It doesn’t take away our productive ability, but it feeds our natural laziness and liberates us from all those small tasks that until now at least partially channeled our productive energy. These energies are now stagnating. And as the good old Seneca wrote, those soldiers who have no work begin to destruct their surroundings and create disturbance. And this is a very important element of the general neurosis we can observe in society

comment.body:
by this I mean that anything whose truth value you can derive cannot be the "absolute truth". 
Why? bcs you derive that truth value through a chosen logical system, from a specific set of axioms. now, it is provable and easily demonstrable that every logical system we create is necessarily incomplete, and every axiom system is necessarily finite. therefore, nothing that can be derived from an axiom system along any logical pathway can ever be equivalent to absolute truth. 
we determine the value of any given statement based on only what percentage of the phenomena we know it applies to
